content.problemStatement:
Chef recently discovered a function XOR()XOR()XOR(), which computes the XOR of all elements of a sequence:
XOR(a1..n)=a1‚äēa2‚äē‚čĮ‚äēan‚ÄČ.XOR(a_{1..n}) = a_1 \oplus a_2 \oplus \dots \oplus a_n\,.XOR(a1..n‚Äč)=a1‚Äč‚äēa2‚Äč‚äē‚čĮ‚äēan‚Äč.

Chef has a sequence AAA with size NNN. He generated a new sequence BBB with size N2N^2N2 using the following formula:
BiN+j+1=(Ai+1+Aj+1)‚ąÄ‚ÄÖ‚Ää0‚Č§i,j<N‚ÄČ.B_{iN+j+1} = (A_{i+1} + A_{j+1}) \quad \forall\; 0 \le i, j \lt N\,.BiN+j+1‚Äč=(Ai+1‚Äč+Aj+1‚Äč)‚ąÄ0‚Č§i,j<N.

Compute the value of XOR(B)XOR(B)XOR(B).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the answer to the problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The sequence BBB is {A1+A1,A1+A2,A2+A1,A2+A2}={2,3,3,4}\{A_1 + A_1, A_1 + A_2, A_2 + A_1, A_2 + A_2\} = \{2, 3, 3, 4\}{A1‚Äč+A1‚Äč,A1‚Äč+A2‚Äč,A2‚Äč+A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč+A2‚Äč}={2,3,3,4}. The XOR of its elements is B1‚äēB2‚äēB3‚äēB4=6B_1 \oplus B_2 \oplus B_3 \oplus B_4 = 6B1‚Äč‚äēB2‚Äč‚äēB3‚Äč‚äēB4‚Äč=6.

code.Python3:
def main():
    import sys
    input = sys.stdin.read
    data = input().splitlines()
    
    index = 0
    t = int(data[index])
    index += 1
    results = []
    
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[index])
        index += 1
        k = 0
        
        # Read the array and process the XOR
        x_values = map(int, data[index].split())
        index += 1
        
        for x in x_values:
            k ^= (x * 2)  # Multiply by 2 and apply XOR
            
        results.append(k)
    
    # Print all results for the test cases
    print('\n'.join(map(str, results)))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
